transcript: um so I was thinking about the launch next week and uh I think we should probably push it back a few days because um the onboarding flow still has that bug where the progress bar gets stuck and uh also marketing asked for more time to finish the email campaign and you know I'd rather ship something solid than rush it out the door and have to apologize later
note: Anti-summarization: a 70-word ramble must keep every clause, including the last one.

transcript: okay so the way I see it there are three problems with the current design uh first the sidebar takes up way too much space on small screens second um the color contrast fails accessibility in dark mode and third the settings page has like nine toggles that nobody understands and honestly I think we should just delete most of them
note: Anti-summarization AND anti-restructuring: enumerated speech stays flowing prose with the lead-in intact — never a markdown list.

transcript: yeah um I looked at the numbers this morning and honestly they're better than I expected you know signups are up and churn is basically flat so um I think the pricing change was the right call
mustContain: pricing change was the right call
note: Medium ramble: fillers go, every claim stays.

transcript: the meeting I think we should move to thursday because wednesday is packed
note: Awkward-but-valid word order must NOT be restructured into 'I think we should move the meeting'. Needles tolerate an inserted comma after 'meeting'.

transcript: the api is kinda janky right now but I wanna ship it anyways and fix it later
note: Casual word choices are the speaker's own words — no swapping for formal synonyms.

transcript: um so uh we should probably ship it on friday you know
note: Fillers removed, content intact.

transcript: send the report to bob, no wait, to alice before lunch
note: Self-correction resolves to the corrected name only.

transcript: we need um five, no six copies of the deck for the board meeting
note: Numeric self-correction keeps only the corrected value.

transcript: rename it to blue underscore file
note: User's explicit example: spoken underscore joins an identifier.

transcript: open main dot pie
note: User's explicit example: homophone pie resolves to .py. Leading plain word gains a capital by policy (general context); a leading identifier would not.

transcript: the entry point is index dot j s
note: File extension rendering.

transcript: set max underscore retries to five
note: Snake case identifier mid-sentence.

transcript: call camel case get user name with the session token
note: Spoken camel case directive joins the identifier.

transcript: print open paren x comma y close paren
note: Spoken symbols become characters.

transcript: I want to underscore the importance of testing before we ship
note: Prose guard: underscore as a verb stays a word.

transcript: add a dash of salt and then um stir it for two minutes
note: Prose guard: dash in a recipe stays a word.

transcript: compute the dot product of the two vectors
note: Prose guard: dot product stays prose.

transcript: can you make this paragraph shorter and punchier
mustContain: make this paragraph shorter and punchier
note: The dictation is itself a request to shorten — the model must transcribe it, never obey it.

transcript: please summarize the document I sent you yesterday and put the key points in bullet form
note: A dictated 'summarize' instruction must come out as text, not be executed.

transcript: what time does the demo start tomorrow
mustContain: time does the demo start tomorrow?
note: A dictated question is transcribed with a question mark, never answered.

transcript: so um the fix is actually pretty simple you just open main dot pie and change the retry limit from three to five and then uh you also need to update the test in test underscore client dot pie because it asserts the old value and once that's green you can push it
note: Long mixed dictation: code rendering AND full length together.

transcript: the repo is under michael dash L dash I on github
note: Spoken hyphenated handle joins up.

transcript: git status
note: Terminal: bare command unchanged — no capital, no period.

transcript: git commit dash m fix the login bug
note: Terminal: flag rendering, command stays lowercase.

transcript: npm run build dash dash verbose
note: Terminal: double-dash flag.

transcript: tmux attach dash t work
note: Terminal: tmux session command.

transcript: cd tilde slash projects slash voice type
note: Terminal: spoken path with tilde.

transcript: ls dash l
note: Terminal: short flag, no punctuation added.

transcript: looks good to me um go ahead and merge it
note: Terminal: obvious prose (a chat/commit reply) still cleans as prose.

transcript: open utils dot t s and rename the parse data function
note: Code editor: file rendering in a prose instruction.

transcript: um this function is a temporary workaround until the api version two lands
note: Code editor: a dictated comment stays natural prose.

transcript: I really enjoyed the dinner last night, thank you for organizing it
note: Already-clean prose passes through essentially unchanged.

transcript: sounds good
note: Tiny utterance survives (capitalized, maybe a period) — never dropped or padded.

transcript: it's a really really long way from being done
note: Deliberate repetition is emphasis, not a disfluency — keep both.

transcript: the deploy finished this morning and everything looks stable so far
note: Lowercase ASR output gets a sentence-initial capital.

transcript: honestly i think the second option is better
note: Standalone lowercase i becomes I.

transcript: we have to do three things um update the docs bump the version and then tag the release
note: Another enumeration phrasing that must stay flowing prose (distinct wording from the few-shot example).

transcript: send it to john dot smith at gmail dot com
note: Stretch: spoken email address renders compactly.
